quoted_tweet.text:
Our latest release has *in-conversation fast language model choice*!

This lets you switch LLMs by:

1. A dropdown or /model slash command
2. Telling the model "switch to this LLM now", it will call a tool for you!

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

tweets.text: Finally, since we have been doing the index for a while now, we’re starting to get a picture of how models evolve over time! It seems that Claude is consistently a few months ahead of the trend, followed by GPT, and then the others in the pack. https://t.co/2joDGWVbll

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We also measure time to resolution for issues, since having quick responses can be important. Here we can see that the Claude and GPT models perform the best — they’re able to use effective parallel tool calling and efficient inference to finish problems quickly. https://t.co/dVyxFCh4oT

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: First, cost/performance, our main graph. We can see that overall Opus 4.7 takes the top spot, although GPT-5.5 is very competitive (it’s the OpenAI logo right next to Opus). GPT-5.4 does very well at a bit of a cheaper price point, and Gemini 3.1 Pro does well at even cheaper. Our least expensive contender DeepSeek-3.2-Thinker is very solid at 1/10 the price of Claude.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We have added a bunch of new models representing the big frontier labs like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google DeepMind, as well as startups like DeepSeek, https://t.co/dXFPfOOl3T, and Minimax, and new entrants such as NVIDIA. Let’s look at the results!

quoted_tweet.text:
🎙️In this episode, we speak with @rajistics, an Agentic AI Engineer at @OpenHandsDev, about the agentic harness behind modern AI systems, which entails the retrieval, memory, tools, orchestration, and execution environment that determine whether an AI agent can work reliably in practice. 

Rajiv shares practical insights on context engineering, agentic RAG, memory design, orchestration patterns, and the tradeoffs teams need to understand when building reliable AI agents.

🎧 Listen to the full episode here → https://t.co/qsRAuCedcK

Rajiv shares practical insights on context engineering, agentic RAG, memory design, orchestration patterns, and the tradeoffs teams need to understand when building reliable AI agents.

quoted_tweet.text:
Our latest release has *in-conversation fast language model choice*!

This lets you switch LLMs by:

1. A dropdown or /model slash command
2. Telling the model "switch to this LLM now", it will call a tool for you!

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

tweets.text: Finally, since we have been doing the index for a while now, we’re starting to get a picture of how models evolve over time! It seems that Claude is consistently a few months ahead of the trend, followed by GPT, and then the others in the pack. https://t.co/2joDGWVbll

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We also measure time to resolution for issues, since having quick responses can be important. Here we can see that the Claude and GPT models perform the best — they’re able to use effective parallel tool calling and efficient inference to finish problems quickly. https://t.co/dVyxFCh4oT

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: First, cost/performance, our main graph. We can see that overall Opus 4.7 takes the top spot, although GPT-5.5 is very competitive (it’s the OpenAI logo right next to Opus). GPT-5.4 does very well at a bit of a cheaper price point, and Gemini 3.1 Pro does well at even cheaper. Our least expensive contender DeepSeek-3.2-Thinker is very solid at 1/10 the price of Claude.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We have added a bunch of new models representing the big frontier labs like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google DeepMind, as well as startups like DeepSeek, https://t.co/dXFPfOOl3T, and Minimax, and new entrants such as NVIDIA. Let’s look at the results!

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

🎙️In this episode, we speak with @rajistics, an Agentic AI Engineer at @OpenHandsDev, about the agentic harness behind modern AI systems, which entails the retrieval, memory, tools, orchestration, and execution environment that determine whether an AI agent can work reliably in practice. 

Rajiv shares practical insights on context engineering, agentic RAG, memory design, orchestration patterns, and the tradeoffs teams need to understand when building reliable AI agents.

quoted_tweet.text:
Our latest release has *in-conversation fast language model choice*!

This lets you switch LLMs by:

1. A dropdown or /model slash command
2. Telling the model "switch to this LLM now", it will call a tool for you!

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

tweets.text: Finally, since we have been doing the index for a while now, we’re starting to get a picture of how models evolve over time! It seems that Claude is consistently a few months ahead of the trend, followed by GPT, and then the others in the pack. https://t.co/2joDGWVbll

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We also measure time to resolution for issues, since having quick responses can be important. Here we can see that the Claude and GPT models perform the best — they’re able to use effective parallel tool calling and efficient inference to finish problems quickly. https://t.co/dVyxFCh4oT

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: First, cost/performance, our main graph. We can see that overall Opus 4.7 takes the top spot, although GPT-5.5 is very competitive (it’s the OpenAI logo right next to Opus). GPT-5.4 does very well at a bit of a cheaper price point, and Gemini 3.1 Pro does well at even cheaper. Our least expensive contender DeepSeek-3.2-Thinker is very solid at 1/10 the price of Claude.

1. A dropdown or /model slash command

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We also measure time to resolution for issues, since having quick responses can be important. Here we can see that the Claude and GPT models perform the best — they’re able to use effective parallel tool calling and efficient inference to finish problems quickly. https://t.co/dVyxFCh4oT

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: First, cost/performance, our main graph. We can see that overall Opus 4.7 takes the top spot, although GPT-5.5 is very competitive (it’s the OpenAI logo right next to Opus). GPT-5.4 does very well at a bit of a cheaper price point, and Gemini 3.1 Pro does well at even cheaper. Our least expensive contender DeepSeek-3.2-Thinker is very solid at 1/10 the price of Claude.

quoted_tweet.text:
🎙️In this episode, we speak with @rajistics, an Agentic AI Engineer at @OpenHandsDev, about the agentic harness behind modern AI systems, which entails the retrieval, memory, tools, orchestration, and execution environment that determine whether an AI agent can work reliably in practice. 

Rajiv shares practical insights on context engineering, agentic RAG, memory design, orchestration patterns, and the tradeoffs teams need to understand when building reliable AI agents.

🎧 Listen to the full episode here → https://t.co/qsRAuCedcK

quoted_tweet.text:
Our latest release has *in-conversation fast language model choice*!

This lets you switch LLMs by:

1. A dropdown or /model slash command
2. Telling the model "switch to this LLM now", it will call a tool for you!

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

tweets.text: Finally, since we have been doing the index for a while now, we’re starting to get a picture of how models evolve over time! It seems that Claude is consistently a few months ahead of the trend, followed by GPT, and then the others in the pack. https://t.co/2joDGWVbll

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We also measure time to resolution for issues, since having quick responses can be important. Here we can see that the Claude and GPT models perform the best — they’re able to use effective parallel tool calling and efficient inference to finish problems quickly. https://t.co/dVyxFCh4oT

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: First, cost/performance, our main graph. We can see that overall Opus 4.7 takes the top spot, although GPT-5.5 is very competitive (it’s the OpenAI logo right next to Opus). GPT-5.4 does very well at a bit of a cheaper price point, and Gemini 3.1 Pro does well at even cheaper. Our least expensive contender DeepSeek-3.2-Thinker is very solid at 1/10 the price of Claude.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We have added a bunch of new models representing the big frontier labs like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google DeepMind, as well as startups like DeepSeek, https://t.co/dXFPfOOl3T, and Minimax, and new entrants such as NVIDIA. Let’s look at the results!

quoted_tweet.text:
🎙️In this episode, we speak with @rajistics, an Agentic AI Engineer at @OpenHandsDev, about the agentic harness behind modern AI systems, which entails the retrieval, memory, tools, orchestration, and execution environment that determine whether an AI agent can work reliably in practice. 

Rajiv shares practical insights on context engineering, agentic RAG, memory design, orchestration patterns, and the tradeoffs teams need to understand when building reliable AI agents.

🎧 Listen to the full episode here → https://t.co/qsRAuCedcK

profile_bio.description: Bringing together the global data science community to help foster the exchange of innovative ideas and encourage the growth of open source software.

Congratulations to @NVIDIAAI on the release of Nemotron 3 Ultra, an exciting step forward in fully open language models.

Nemotron Ultra was trained to work well with OpenHands, and we are happy to provide tier-1 support for it!

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

quoted_tweet.text:
Today we're shipping Nemotron 3 Ultra.

A 550B MoE frontier-intelligence open model built for long-running agents.

It delivers 5x faster inference and lowers the cost of complex agentic tasks by up to 30% versus other open frontier models. https://t.co/FEXqvfzQFO

profile_bio.description: Teaching your AI new tricks.

tweets.text:
We have also partnered with @MiniMax_AI to provide

*free access to agents with MiniMax-M2.7*

for a limited time!

This is a great opportunity to test out how to mix in cost-effective models into your coding workflows.

Try it out here: https://t.co/FQw0hqxzLX

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

quoted_tweet.text:
Our latest release has *in-conversation fast language model choice*!

This lets you switch LLMs by:

1. A dropdown or /model slash command
2. Telling the model "switch to this LLM now", it will call a tool for you!

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

tweets.text: Finally, since we have been doing the index for a while now, we’re starting to get a picture of how models evolve over time! It seems that Claude is consistently a few months ahead of the trend, followed by GPT, and then the others in the pack. https://t.co/2joDGWVbll

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We also measure time to resolution for issues, since having quick responses can be important. Here we can see that the Claude and GPT models perform the best — they’re able to use effective parallel tool calling and efficient inference to finish problems quickly. https://t.co/dVyxFCh4oT

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: First, cost/performance, our main graph. We can see that overall Opus 4.7 takes the top spot, although GPT-5.5 is very competitive (it’s the OpenAI logo right next to Opus). GPT-5.4 does very well at a bit of a cheaper price point, and Gemini 3.1 Pro does well at even cheaper. Our least expensive contender DeepSeek-3.2-Thinker is very solid at 1/10 the price of Claude.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We have added a bunch of new models representing the big frontier labs like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google DeepMind, as well as startups like DeepSeek, https://t.co/dXFPfOOl3T, and Minimax, and new entrants such as NVIDIA. Let’s look at the results!

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

quoted_tweet.text:
🎙️In this episode, we speak with @rajistics, an Agentic AI Engineer at @OpenHandsDev, about the agentic harness behind modern AI systems, which entails the retrieval, memory, tools, orchestration, and execution environment that determine whether an AI agent can work reliably in practice. 

Rajiv shares practical insights on context engineering, agentic RAG, memory design, orchestration patterns, and the tradeoffs teams need to understand when building reliable AI agents.

🎧 Listen to the full episode here → https://t.co/qsRAuCedcK

profile_bio.description: Bringing together the global data science community to help foster the exchange of innovative ideas and encourage the growth of open source software.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

quoted_tweet.text:
Today we're shipping Nemotron 3 Ultra.

A 550B MoE frontier-intelligence open model built for long-running agents.

It delivers 5x faster inference and lowers the cost of complex agentic tasks by up to 30% versus other open frontier models. https://t.co/FEXqvfzQFO

profile_bio.description: Teaching your AI new tricks.

tweets.text:
We have also partnered with @MiniMax_AI to provide

*free access to agents with MiniMax-M2.7*

for a limited time!

This is a great opportunity to test out how to mix in cost-effective models into your coding workflows.

Try it out here: https://t.co/FQw0hqxzLX

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

quoted_tweet.text:
Our latest release has *in-conversation fast language model choice*!

This lets you switch LLMs by:

1. A dropdown or /model slash command
2. Telling the model "switch to this LLM now", it will call a tool for you!

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

Our latest release has *in-conversation fast language model choice*!

This lets you switch LLMs by:

1. A dropdown or /model slash command
2. Telling the model "switch to this LLM now", it will call a tool for you!

You can say things like "use opus for planning, minimax for implementation, and gpt for final review".

tweets.text: Finally, since we have been doing the index for a while now, we’re starting to get a picture of how models evolve over time! It seems that Claude is consistently a few months ahead of the trend, followed by GPT, and then the others in the pack. https://t.co/2joDGWVbll

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: Let’s look at the task-by-task breakdown. For resolving issues on Python repos (SWE-Bench), it’s a competitive field, with many models getting good results. For greenfield, long-horizon development, we see Claude and GPT take the show, they are very strong at performing big, long-running tasks. For frontend development, Claude and Gemini are strong, reflecting their strong multimodal capabilities and the new Claude Opus 4.7 stands out from the pack. But GLM-5.1 also takes close to a top place, a strong contender in where open models have struggled previously. For software testing, Claude is the run-away winner, it seems to have very strong debugging skills! And finally for information gathering, GPT takes the top, although Claude also has a strong showing.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We also measure time to resolution for issues, since having quick responses can be important. Here we can see that the Claude and GPT models perform the best — they’re able to use effective parallel tool calling and efficient inference to finish problems quickly. https://t.co/dVyxFCh4oT

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: First, cost/performance, our main graph. We can see that overall Opus 4.7 takes the top spot, although GPT-5.5 is very competitive (it’s the OpenAI logo right next to Opus). GPT-5.4 does very well at a bit of a cheaper price point, and Gemini 3.1 Pro does well at even cheaper. Our least expensive contender DeepSeek-3.2-Thinker is very solid at 1/10 the price of Claude.

profile_bio.description: OpenHands is the leading open source agent for software development, usable through a CLI, GUI, SDK, or IDE https://t.co/LvSlDFkAwA

tweets.text: We have added a bunch of new models representing the big frontier labs like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google DeepMind, as well as startups like DeepSeek, https://t.co/dXFPfOOl3T, and Minimax, and new entrants such as NVIDIA. Let’s look at the results!

quoted_tweet.text:
🎙️In this episode, we speak with @rajistics, an Agentic AI Engineer at @OpenHandsDev, about the agentic harness behind modern AI systems, which entails the retrieval, memory, tools, orchestration, and execution environment that determine whether an AI agent can work reliably in practice. 

Rajiv shares practical insights on context engineering, agentic RAG, memory design, orchestration patterns, and the tradeoffs teams need to understand when building reliable AI agents.

🎧 Listen to the full episode here → https://t.co/qsRAuCedcK
